steps: Have your drafted LCSR narrative ready in a file, with every number in it already sourced back to your own records.
In Claude Code, turn on agent teams in settings if it isn't already on — this feature is experimental.
Say the words 'team create' out loud in your prompt — otherwise Claude will quietly hand you ordinary sub-agents instead of a team.
Give the team distinct seats: a skeptical grant reviewer, a community member who doesn't already listen to your station, your GM, and a station down the road doing this well.
Hand the team your narrative and let them debate it across a couple of rounds until they reach a consensus.
Read back what they agreed was persuasive and what they agreed was filler.
Revise the narrative using their consensus, but verify every number in the revised version against your own records yourself.
Watch your usage as you go — a team that talks to itself for multiple rounds spends against your five-hour session limit fast.
verify: Take every statistic the team's consensus praised as persuasive and trace it back to your own records by hand. An agent team will reach a confident consensus about a number it invented just as easily as one that's real.
